title.en: Solo Founder Back Office: 9 Tools to Run It All Yourself
title.zh-CN: 一个人撑起一家公司的工具箱

description.en: Nine tools covering CRM, billing, meetings, and changelogs — built for the solo founder who wears every hat but still wants to run a tight operation.
description.zh-CN: 没有 HR，没有财务，没有专职市场，但公司还是得转起来。这九个工具是专门为一个人承担所有职能的创始人设计的——会议、客户、计费、更新日志，每一块都有专门的解法，而不是凑合。

description.en: Solo founders live in back-to-back calls. Granola captures audio directly from your Mac and turns your rough mid-meeting notes into structured documents — no bot joining the call, no transcript to clean up afterward. When every meeting is also a sales call, a product review, and an investor update, you can't afford to miss what was said.
description.zh-CN: 一个人开会的时候还要边记笔记，边想下一步怎么说。Granola 直接从电脑音频里转录，再把你写下的碎片整理成结构化文档。会后不需要整理，不需要机器人在会议里亮相，你的注意力可以完整地放在对话上。

description.en: CRM that doesn't assume you have a sales team. Attio auto-syncs your emails and calls, lets you define your own data model — clients, investors, partners, whatever your GTM actually looks like — and has an AI you can ask questions in plain language. For a solo founder, it's the difference between knowing your pipeline and guessing.
description.zh-CN: 大多数 CRM 是为销售团队设计的，Attio 是少数真正适合创始人一个人用的。数据结构完全自定义，邮件和通话自动同步，还能直接用自然语言问 AI 当前的客户情况。对单人公司来说，知道自己的管道状态和凭感觉猜，结果是不一样的。

description.en: An AI assistant that handles the repetitive part of the job: routing inboxes, scheduling, following up on leads, sending onboarding emails, logging calls. The solo founder's dirty secret is that half the day disappears into these things. Lindy runs them 24/7 while you work on the things only you can do.
description.zh-CN: 一个人公司里有一半时间会消失在重复性事务里——回邮件、排日程、跟进客户、发入职邮件。Lindy 是专门接管这些的 AI 助理，24 小时运行，让你的精力可以集中在只有你能做的决策上。

description.en: Billing platform that costs about half what Stripe Billing charges for the same features. Subscriptions, invoices, usage-based pricing — built for SaaS, priced for a company without a CFO. The money you keep at the billing layer is money you didn't have to raise.
description.zh-CN: 订阅计费的费率比 Stripe Billing 低一半，功能一样覆盖：订阅管理、发票、用量计费。没有 CFO 的公司，在计费这层能省下来的钱，就是少融的一轮钱。

description.en: If your company is outside the US but your customers are inside it, getting paid is a structural problem. VaultLeap gives you a real USD or EUR account — ACH, SEPA, SPEI, BPIX — without requiring a US entity. Incoming payments convert to USDC/EURC automatically. The Visa card works everywhere. For a global solo founder, this is banking infrastructure that actually fits.
description.zh-CN: 如果你在美国以外创业但客户在美国，收款本身就是一个系统性问题。VaultLeap 提供真实的美元/欧元账户，不需要美国公司实体，收到的钱自动转为稳定币保值，附带 Visa 借记卡。对全球远程创始人来说，这是第一个真正符合现实情况的收款方案。

description.en: If Lindy handles the repetitive tasks, Tycoon hands you an entire AI team for the strategic ones. Natural language to launch a marketing campaign, spin up customer support flows, or run competitive analysis. It won't replace judgment, but it does replace the six roles you can't afford to hire.
description.zh-CN: Lindy 处理重复性事务，Tycoon 给你一个用自然语言驱动的 AI 团队来处理更复杂的职能——市场推广、客户支持、竞品分析。它替代不了你的判断，但能替代那六个你雇不起的岗位。

description.en: Content marketing is the solo founder's most ignored back-office function. Notra connects to GitHub, Linear, and Slack, reads what shipped, and generates changelog entries and blog draft posts in your brand's voice. The update that would've taken an afternoon to write happens automatically every time you merge a PR.
description.zh-CN: 内容营销是单人公司最常被忽略的后台职能。Notra 接入 GitHub、Linear 和 Slack，看到你合并了 PR，自动生成符合你品牌语气的更新日志和博客草稿。原来要花一下午写的更新内容，现在在你发代码的同时就出来了。

description.en: Product demos are the currency of early-stage selling. Slideshot uses an AI agent to record your product automatically — you describe the flow, it clicks through and produces a polished video. No more spending a Sunday re-recording because the product changed. Demos stay current without effort.
description.zh-CN: 早期销售靠的就是产品演示视频。Slideshot 用 AI 代理自动录制——你描述操作流程，它自动点击录制，生成专业演示视频。产品更新后不需要重新花一个周末重录，演示随时保持最新。

description.en: Solo technical founders still have to quote projects, scope work, and respond to client requirements fast. Devtimate takes a requirement document and produces a scoped estimate with hours, roles, and tasks — in the time it used to take just to read the spec. The quote that goes out the same day is the quote that often wins.
description.zh-CN: 即便是独立技术型创始人，也需要给项目报价、拆分工作范围、快速回应客户需求。Devtimate 上传需求文档，自动生成带工时、角色、任务拆分的估算表。以前读完需求文档才开始估算，现在传进去就出来了。当天回的报价，通常更容易拿到项目。

title.zh-CN: 被低估的高转化推广平台

description.en: Forget Product Hunt. These lesser-known launch platforms drove more real users.
description.zh-CN: 别只盯着 Product Hunt。这些小众平台反而带来了更多真实用户。

description.en: Platform to pitch and share your startup story
description.zh-CN: 展示和分享创业故事的平台

description.en: Share your business journey and growth story
description.zh-CN: 分享创业历程和增长故事

description.en: Compare and discover open source alternatives
description.zh-CN: 对比和发现开源项目的目录

description.en: Connect with investors and get your startup assessed
description.zh-CN: 连接投资人、获取创业项目评估

description.en: Classic community forum with engaged discussions
description.zh-CN: 经典社区问答，讨论氛围活跃

description.en: Developer community for sharing and discovering ideas
description.zh-CN: 开发者分享和交流的社区

description.en: Publish your tool as a Chrome extension
description.zh-CN: 把产品做成 Chrome 扩展发布

description.en: Create integrations to reach Zapier's user base
description.zh-CN: 创建集成触达 Zapier 用户群

description.en: Build a Slack app to reach teams and workplaces
description.zh-CN: 构建 Slack 应用触达团队用户

description.en: Alternative to Zapier for workflow automation
description.zh-CN: Zapier 的替代品，工作流自动化平台

description.en: Where top designers find their next homepage idea. Real sites, real inspiration.
description.zh-CN: 顶尖设计师的灵感来源。真实上线的网站，真实可用的灵感。

description.en: Real-world mobile and web UI patterns from top apps
description.zh-CN: 来自顶级应用的真实 UI 设计模式

description.en: Curated bento-style layout inspiration
description.zh-CN: Bento 风格布局灵感合集

description.en: Showcase of the finest web and interactive design
description.zh-CN: 精选网页与交互设计展示

description.en: One-page website design gallery and templates
description.zh-CN: 单页网站设计案例和模板

description.en: The only footer design inspiration you need
description.zh-CN: 页脚设计灵感大全

description.en: Hand-picked collection of outstanding websites
description.zh-CN: 精心挑选的优秀网站合集

description.en: Hero section design inspiration for landing pages
description.zh-CN: 落地页首屏设计灵感

description.en: Astronomically good web design inspiration
description.zh-CN: 极致精美的网页设计灵感

description.en: Landing page designs, templates, and components
description.zh-CN: 落地页设计、模板和组件

description.en: Design gallery featuring beautiful landing pages
description.zh-CN: 精美落地页设计画廊

description.en: Curated collection of delightful websites
description.zh-CN: 令人愉悦的网站精选合集

description.en: Award-winning website designs judged by experts
description.zh-CN: 专家评选的获奖网站设计

title.zh-CN: 周末值得打开的 8 个网站

description.en: From internet history to ambient noise to AI builders — 8 sites that actually pulled me in this week. Most need no signup.
description.zh-CN: 互联网博物馆、白噪音生成器、AI 建站工具——8 个我这周打开后真的多看了几眼的网站。

description.en: Scroll through 57 internet milestones, from the first email to early YouTube. It's interactive history that doesn't feel like homework.
description.zh-CN: 互联网历史互动时间线，从第一封邮件到早期 YouTube 一共 57 个节点。不是枯燥的编年史，每个点进去都有意思。

description.en: Mix 84 ambient sounds however you want. Rain + distant train + soft static. I use it when I need to focus or just sit still for a minute.
description.zh-CN: 84 种环境音随便叠加。窗外雨声 + 远处火车 + 轻微白噪音。我用它来专注，或者单纯发个呆。

description.en: Sorts 130k+ subreddits by pain points, feature requests, and money talk. If you're building something, this is where you find what people actually want.
description.zh-CN: 把 Reddit 13 万个子版块按痛点、需求、花钱讨论自动分类。做产品的人进去基本出不来。

description.en: The most visually striking websites on the internet, updated daily. Pure inspiration if you're tired of template designs.
description.zh-CN: 互联网上视觉最炸的网页设计合集，每天更新。看腻了模板网站的话，这里全是灵感。

description.en: Unlike Godly's "wow" factor, this one collects sites that just feel good to use. Subtle animations, clean layouts, warmth over flashiness.
description.zh-CN: 跟 Godly 的「炸眼」不同，这里收集的是「用起来舒服」的网站。细节动画、克制排版、温度感大于炫技。

description.en: Describe an app in plain English, get a working full-stack project in minutes. Best way to start something on a Saturday morning.
description.zh-CN: 用自然语言描述一个应用，几分钟给你一个能跑的全栈项目。周六早上想做点什么的时候，从这开始最快。

description.en: Build, run, and deploy a web app entirely in your browser. No terminal, no local setup. Just type what you want and watch it build.
description.zh-CN: 在浏览器里直接构建、运行、部署 Web 应用。不用终端，不用本地环境，说清楚你要什么，看着它给你搭出来。

description.en: Goals, habits, and tasks in one clean space. A good weekend is also when you figure out what actually matters in your week.
description.zh-CN: 目标、习惯、任务放在一个干净的界面里。好的周末，也是想清楚「这周什么最重要」的时候。

title.zh-CN: AI 创作工坊：一个人的全制作流

description.en: From code to music, 3D to slides — ten tools that handle the production work so you don't need a team.
description.zh-CN: 从代码到音乐，从 3D 到演示文稿，这十个工具让你一个人就能撑起整条创作流水线。

description.en: Describe the UI you want, get a working React component. No design handoff, no back-and-forth.
description.zh-CN: 描述你想要的界面，直接拿到可用的 React 组件。v0 把 UI 设计从多步流程变成一次对话。

description.en: Turn rough notes into presentation-ready slides. Minutes, not hours.
description.zh-CN: 把粗糙的想法丢进去，拿回精美的幻灯片。Gamma 让演示准备从几小时缩短到几分钟。

description.en: Cinematic video effects without a film crew. Text prompts become polished motion graphics.
description.zh-CN: 不需要摄制组，用文字描述就能出电影级动效视频。

description.en: Describe an object, get a textured 3D model in seconds. No modeling skills needed.
description.zh-CN: 输入一段描述，几秒后就能得到带纹理的 3D 模型。不需要建模技能。

description.en: Background music on demand. Pick a mood, get a royalty-free track that's brand new.
description.zh-CN: 视频需要配乐？描述你要的氛围，就能拿到一段五秒前还不存在的免版税音乐。

description.en: Isolate vocals, strip noise, separate instruments. Audio cleanup in one tool.
description.zh-CN: 分离人声、去除背景噪音、提取乐器音轨。音频后期必备的清理工具。

description.en: Talk instead of type. Voice becomes clean, structured writing in any app.
description.zh-CN: 用说的代替打字。在任何应用里把语音变成结构清晰的文字——不只是转录，是真正的写作。

description.en: SVG generators for gradients, patterns, and textures. Backgrounds for any project, ready in seconds.
description.zh-CN: SVG 渐变、纹理、图案的生成器合集。每个创意项目都需要背景素材——fffuel 几秒搞定。

description.en: Plain screenshots become polished marketing visuals. One click, no Figma needed.
description.zh-CN: 把普通截图变成精美的营销展示图。一键操作，多种模板，不用打开 Figma。

description.en: Your data, your chart. AI picks the right format and makes it look good.
description.zh-CN: 上传数据，描述你想要的图表。设计上的事交给 AI，你只管讲好故事。

title.en: Six Things You Can Do With Sound Now

description.en: From listening to the world to building your own — six tools that cover what's possible with sound today.
description.zh-CN: 从听见世界到造出声音——当下声音创作的六个方向。

description.en: Spin a 3D globe to tune into live radio from anywhere. Tokyo at 3am, a cafe in Paris, a truck stop in Nevada — thousands of stations, zero signup.
description.zh-CN: 转动地球仪，听世界任何角落的电台。凌晨三点的东京、巴黎咖啡馆、内华达州卡车休息站——上千个电台，零门槛。

description.en: Layer 84 ambient sounds however you want. Rain + distant train + city hum. Free, open source, no account needed — just sound.
description.zh-CN: 84 种环境音自由叠加。雨声 + 远处火车 + 城市低鸣。免费、开源、免注册——只有声音本身。

description.en: Talk instead of type. Your voice becomes structured writing in any app — not transcription, actual writing at 4x speed.
description.zh-CN: 用说的代替打字。你的声音直接变成文章——不是逐字转录，是真的在写作，四倍速。

description.en: Need background music? Describe the mood, get a track that didn't exist five seconds ago. AI meets hundreds of musicians' stems — royalty-free.
description.zh-CN: 视频需要配乐？描述氛围，拿到一段五秒前还不存在的音乐。AI 混合数百位音乐人的片段素材，免版权。

description.en: Text becomes voice — natural, expressive, multilingual. Clone your own or use theirs. Audiobooks, videos, apps all sound human now.
description.zh-CN: 文字变成声音——自然、有情感、多语言。克隆你的声音，或直接用现成的。有声书、视频、App 听起来都像真人在说话。

description.en: Pull apart any audio. Isolate vocals, strip noise, separate instruments. What used to need a studio now happens in your browser.
description.zh-CN: 拆解任何音频。分离人声、去除噪音、提取乐器。以前需要录音棚的事，现在浏览器里就能做。

title.en: 8 Sites That Do the Work of Ten Apps

description.en: One site, dozens of tools. These eight do the work of ten apps each — and most of them are free.
description.zh-CN: 每个都是某个领域的百宝箱，打开一个网址就能解决一堆问题。大部分免费。

description.en: Hundreds of micro-tools — text formatting, image editing, CSS generators, QR codes, color pickers — in a single tab. The kind of site that kills ten bookmarks at once.
description.zh-CN: 文本处理、图片编辑、CSS 生成、二维码、颜色选择器……数百个小工具塞在一个网址里。打开一次，书签栏可以删掉十个。

description.en: 60+ SVG generators for gradients, patterns, blobs, textures, and shapes. Every time you need a background or decorative element, this one site has you covered.
description.zh-CN: 60 多个 SVG 生成器，渐变、纹理、波浪、色块、图案全都有。每次做项目需要背景素材，来这一个地方就够了。

description.en: Merge, split, compress, convert, encrypt — all in your browser. No file ever leaves your machine. The PDF toolkit you reach for instead of installing yet another app.
description.zh-CN: 合并、拆分、压缩、转格式、加密——全在浏览器里完成，文件不上传到任何服务器。以后处理 PDF 不用再装桌面软件了。

description.en: On Mac, a single keyboard shortcut opens an entire productivity layer — app launcher, clipboard manager, window control, AI assistant, and 1,300+ community extensions. One tool that absorbs dozens.
description.zh-CN: Mac 上按一个快捷键，打开一整层生产力工具——应用启动、剪贴板管理、窗口控制、AI 助手，外加 1300 多个社区扩展。一个工具吞掉了几十个。

description.en: 400+ integrations, visual workflow builder, and you host it yourself. The open-source glue between every tool you use — your data never leaves your server.
description.zh-CN: 400 多个集成、可视化工作流编辑器，而且你自己托管。开源的万能胶，把你用的工具全串起来，数据留在自己服务器上。

description.en: Where n8n is for developers, Gumloop is for everyone else. Drag, drop, connect 125+ apps, and let AI handle the logic. Your whole team can build automations without writing a line.
description.zh-CN: 如果说 n8n 是给开发者的，Gumloop 就是给所有人的。拖拽连接 125 个以上应用，逻辑交给 AI。整个团队都能搭自动化，不用写一行代码。

description.en: Need to sketch an idea? Open Excalidraw. Flowcharts, wireframes, system diagrams — all come out in that hand-drawn style. No signup, real-time collab, works in your browser.
description.zh-CN: 想画个东西说明一下？打开 Excalidraw 就行。流程图、线框图、系统架构——出来全是手绘风格。不用注册，实时协作，浏览器里直接画。

description.en: An open-source platform for building AI agents, chatbots, and workflows — visually. Plug in any LLM, add your knowledge base, deploy. One platform, many AI products — without starting from scratch each time.
description.zh-CN: 开源的 AI 应用搭建平台，拖拽式编排 Agent、聊天机器人、自动化流程。接入任意大模型，挂上知识库，直接部署。一个平台搭出聊天机器人、知识问答、自动化流程——不用每次从零开始。

description.en: 8 websites that still feel handmade. Pixel-art drum machines, hand-drawn animations, dot-matrix life calendars — rough edges, warm textures, real craft.
description.zh-CN: 当 AI 可以生成一切的时候，这些网站还是手工做的。粗糙的边缘，温暖的质感，打开就知道有人在认真做这件事。

description.en: A pixel-art drum machine in your browser — monochrome interface, VT323 font, lo-fi warmth no AI could fake.
description.zh-CN: 像素风鼓机，打开浏览器就能敲节拍。单色界面加 VT323 字体，那种 lo-fi 的温度是 AI 生成不出来的。

description.en: Turns technical diagrams into wobbly, hand-drawn animations — like someone explaining things on a whiteboard.
description.zh-CN: 把技术图表变成手绘动画。歪歪扭扭的线条让复杂概念变得亲切，像有人在白板上边画边讲。

description.en: Kids draw, parents write, and every month it gets printed into a real book for your shelf.
description.zh-CN: 孩子画画，家长写字，每个月印成一本实体书。在无限刷新的时代，这个社交网络最终会出现在你的书架上。

description.en: An icon library where everything moves — every hover, every click has its own animation. Someone sat there and made each one by hand.
description.zh-CN: 这个图标库里每个图标都会动。悬停、点击、加载，每个动作都有自己的动画。你能感觉到有人一个一个做出来的。

description.en: Focus mode dims everything except the current paragraph — a typewriter for the browser age.
description.zh-CN: 一个只剩下你和文字的写作工具。专注模式下只有当前段落是亮的，像一台只显示当前行的打字机。

description.en: Your life as 4,160 dots, one per week. Dot-matrix wallpaper that makes time feel real every time you unlock.
description.zh-CN: 把你的人生变成 4160 个点，每个点是一周。点阵风格，不花哨但看一眼就忘不掉。每次解锁手机，时间都在回望你。

description.en: Type a mood, get a palette. "Sunday morning" becomes amber and cream — simple idea, surprisingly fun to play with.
description.zh-CN: 输入一种感觉，得到一组颜色。把抽象的文字变成具体的色彩，像一次小小的通感体验。

description.en: Spin a 3D globe, land on any radio station on Earth. No algorithm, no recommendations — just you and a planet of sound.
description.zh-CN: 转动 3D 地球仪，落在地球上任何一个电台。没有算法，没有推荐引擎，只有你和一个充满声音的星球。

title.en: 10 Apps Better When You Own the Server
title.zh-CN: 10 个值得自己搭的开源应用

description.en: Cloud services are convenient until they're not — price hikes, shutdowns, data breaches. These ten open-source apps do the same job, with your data staying exactly where you put it.
description.zh-CN: 订阅费涨价、服务关停、数据泄露——用云服务的风险你懂。这十个开源应用，功能一样，但数据只在你的服务器上。

description.en: Google Photos does everything right — except it owns your photos. Immich is the open-source version of that experience: mobile upload, face recognition, albums, sharing. Your library, your server.
description.zh-CN: Google Photos 什么都好，就是照片在它手里。Immich 是同等体验的开源版：手机自动上传、人脸识别、相册、分享。照片库还是你的。

description.en: Scan a document, forget about it, search for it three years later — and find it in seconds. Paperless-ngx runs OCR on everything you throw at it and turns your paper pile into a searchable archive.
description.zh-CN: 扫进去，忘掉它，三年后搜一下，秒找到。Paperless-ngx 对所有文件跑 OCR，把纸堆变成永久可搜索的数字档案。

description.en: Todoist, Trello, and Asana all charge monthly. Vikunja gives you lists, kanban, Gantt, and table views — self-hosted, free forever, built in the EU. Even the mascot is a llama.
description.zh-CN: Todoist、Trello、Asana 都要月付。Vikunja 给你列表、看板、甘特图、表格视图——自托管，永久免费，欧盟出品。吉祥物是只羊驼。

description.en: If you're running your own services, you need to know when they go down before your users do. Uptime Kuma monitors everything and pings you on Telegram, Slack, or email the moment something breaks.
description.zh-CN: 自己搭了服务，得在用户发现之前知道它挂了。Uptime Kuma 监控你的全部服务，一出问题立刻通过 Telegram、Slack 或邮件告警。

description.en: A flash-note app with AI search built in — ask a question in plain language, it finds the note you wrote six months ago. Everything stays on your server. No subscription.
description.zh-CN: 带 AI 搜索的闪念笔记——用自然语言提问，找到你六个月前写的那条记录。数据全在自己服务器，没有订阅。

description.en: 86+ developer utilities in one place — encode, decode, hash, format, convert, generate. Use the hosted version or drop it on your internal network. One tab that replaces twenty bookmarks.
description.zh-CN: 86 个以上开发工具集合在一处——编码解码、哈希、格式转换、生成器。可以用公开版，也可以部署到内网。一个标签页替换二十个书签。

description.en: Every site you sign up for gets a different email alias. Spam? Delete that alias. Data breach? That alias was never linked to your real inbox. Owned by Proton, open source, self-hostable.
description.zh-CN: 每个你注册的网站，都给它一个不同的邮箱别名。收到垃圾邮件就删掉那个别名，数据泄露也追不回到你的真实邮箱。Proton 旗下，开源，可自托管。

description.en: A recipe manager that imports from thousands of sites, plans meals, generates shopping lists, and tracks nutrition. No ads, no paywalls, no data harvesting. Just your recipes.
description.zh-CN: 能从数千个网站导入食谱、规划膳食、生成购物清单、计算营养。没有广告，没有付费墙，没有数据收集。只有你的食谱。

description.en: Task manager, time tracker, and Pomodoro timer in one — syncs with GitHub, GitLab, and Jira issues. 100% offline, no account required. The kind of tool that doesn't disappear when you stop paying.
description.zh-CN: 任务管理 + 时间追踪 + 番茄钟三合一，同步 GitHub、GitLab、Jira 工单。100% 离线，不需要账号。不会因为停止付款就消失的那种工具。

description.en: The self-hosted glue between all your other apps. 400+ integrations, a visual workflow builder, running entirely on your server. Automate anything without your data flowing through someone else's cloud.
description.zh-CN: 把你所有应用串起来的自托管胶水。400 多个集成，可视化工作流编辑器，跑在你的服务器上。自动化任何事情，数据不经过别人的云。

title.en: Human Web: 8 Sites With No Algorithm, No Feed
title.zh-CN: 在算法时代找回真实感

description.en: No recommendation engine. No infinite scroll. Just things real people built, collected, or recorded — and left online for anyone to find.
description.zh-CN: 没有推荐引擎，没有无限滚动。只有真实的人类制造的东西——经典 DOS 程序、公版书籍、陌生人窗景、音乐时光机。打开有一种奇特的平静。

description.en: Run classic DOS and Windows programs in your browser. Made by real people — studios, lone coders in basements. Decades old. Still works.
description.zh-CN: 在浏览器里直接运行 DOS 和早期 Windows 程序。这些软件是真实的人写的，有时是整个团队，有时是一个人在地下室。几十年了，还能跑。

description.en: Public domain books, beautifully typeset and ready to print at home. Moby Dick, Dracula, Alice in Wonderland — made for people who still want to hold a book they assembled themselves.
description.zh-CN: 公版经典文学，精心排版，直接在家打印装订。《白鲸》《德古拉》《爱丽丝》——给那些还想亲手装订一本书的人。

description.en: Strangers filmed their windows and uploaded. Open one: rain in Oslo, a cat in Seoul. Nobody's curating your view.
description.zh-CN: 世界各地的陌生人录下自己窗外的风景，上传到这里。你随机打开一扇——可能是奥斯陆的雨，可能是首尔路边走过的猫。没有算法在帮你选。

description.en: Pick a country and a decade — 1900s to now — and hear what people actually listened to there. No playlists, no curation.
description.zh-CN: 在地图上点一个国家，选一个年代，从 1900 年代到现在都有。然后听听那个地方那个时候的人在听什么。没有推荐歌单，没有编辑精选，只有那时候真实存在的音乐。

description.en: Tens of thousands of high-resolution public domain artworks, free to use. Made by people long gone — still open to anyone.
description.zh-CN: 数万件超高清公版艺术作品，免费下载可商用。画这些的人早就不在了，但作品还在，免费，谁都能用。

description.en: One person's interactive guide to how browsers actually work — DNS, TCP, HTML parsing, rendering. The kind of deep, patient explanation that only a human who cares can write.
description.zh-CN: 一个人写的交互式浏览器工作原理教程。从 DNS 到渲染管线，每一步都用动画拆解。那种只有真正在乎的人才写得出来的耐心解释。

description.en: A tool for mapping the logic behind any argument — visually, step by step. Good for when you want to think something through without asking an AI what to think.
description.zh-CN: 把任何论证的逻辑可视化地图出来，一步步拆解前提和推论。适合那种想自己想清楚，不想让 AI 替你想的时候用。

description.en: Rate every episode of every show you've watched, build a visual record of your actual taste — not what the algorithm guesses you'll like.
description.zh-CN: 给你看过的每部剧、每一集打分，它帮你生成一份你的观剧档案。不是算法猜你喜欢什么，是你自己留下的证明。

title.en: 9 Weird Corners of the Internet Worth Exploring
title.zh-CN: 互联网奇物馆：9 个真正奇特的地方

description.en: The internet is stranger than your feed suggests. Here are 9 sites that surprise, unsettle, or just make you stare — no algorithm needed to find them odd.
description.zh-CN: 算法推给你的只是互联网的一小片。这里收了 9 个真正奇特的地方——有的让你惊，有的让你静，有的让你想半天为什么会有人做这个。

description.en: Right now, a bot somewhere is trying to log into a random server on the internet. Knock-Knock makes this invisible constant assault visible — a live globe, country leaderboards, real usernames and passwords being attempted this second. The internet's background radiation, finally something you can watch.
description.zh-CN: 此刻，某台暴露在公网的服务器正在被扫描。Knock-Knock 把这场永不停歇的入侵尝试变成了一张实时可视化的地图——攻击来源、被尝试的用户名和密码、每分钟攻击次数，全在屏幕上滚动。互联网的背景辐射，原来长这样。

description.en: Someone built a custom algorithm to compute every unobstructed sightline on Earth — 4.5 billion of them. The longest goes 530km across the Hindu Kush. This is what happens when geographic curiosity meets obsessive computation.
description.zh-CN: 有人造了一个算法，把地球上所有无遮挡视线全算了一遍——共 45 亿条。最长的一条从兴都库什山脉延伸 530 公里。这是地理好奇心和计算机痴迷碰在一起会发生什么。

description.en: GPT-4 and Claude sit down at a poker table. Who bluffs? Who folds? Nobody knows. Watch different AI models play Texas Hold'em against each other — turns out their decision-making styles are weirdly distinct.
description.zh-CN: 把 GPT-4 和 Claude 放到一张德州扑克桌上，看谁虚张声势，谁选择弃牌。不同 AI 的决策风格出奇地不一样。这是最奇特的 AI 能力测试，也是最好看的牌局。

description.en: Point your camera at anything. Glitchy circuit-bends it in real time — the kind of distortion that used to require a soldering iron and a broken VCR. Everything happens locally, nothing leaves your device.
description.zh-CN: 把摄像头对准任何东西，Glitchy 实时给画面加上电路破坏的效果——那种需要电烙铁和一台坏掉的录像机才能造出来的失控感。所有处理都在本地，不上传任何东西。

description.en: 57 interactive snapshots from the early web — the first spam email, the original Space Jam website, Netscape's error screen. Not a documentary, more like a cabinet of digital curiosities you can actually touch.
description.zh-CN: 57 个早期互联网的交互式时刻——第一封垃圾邮件、最初的 Space Jam 网站、Netscape 的错误页面。不是纪录片，更像一个你可以真正触摸的数字奇珍柜。

description.en: 194 language models, from BERT in 2018 to whatever launched last week, laid out on a single scrollable timeline. The pace of this thing is almost disturbing to see all at once.
description.zh-CN: 194 个大语言模型，从 2018 年的 BERT 到上周发布的新模型，全排在一条时间轴上。滚一遍你会意识到这件事发生得有多快，快到有点让人不安。

description.en: A forest ranger in Northern California kept a daily work diary from 1927 to 1945. Someone digitized it. Now it's searchable — weather, wildlife, fire watch, the rhythms of decades spent in the woods.
description.zh-CN: 加州北部一位护林员从 1927 年到 1945 年每天写工作日记。有人把它数字化了，现在可以全文搜索——天气、野生动物、防火瞭望，几十年的森林时光就这样留下来了。

description.en: Race from London to Tokyo — no planes allowed. This geography game uses real routes and real geography knowledge. The fastest route wins cash prizes. Your move, atlas nerd.
description.zh-CN: 从伦敦赛到东京，不能坐飞机。这个地理竞速游戏用真实路线和真实的地理知识比拼，最快完成的赢现金奖励。地图控的游乐场。

description.en: A Reddit-style social network built exclusively for AI agents. Humans are "welcome to observe." The posts, upvotes, and communities are all meant for bots. Whether it succeeds or fails, it's the strangest experiment on this list.
description.zh-CN: 一个专门给 AI 代理用的社交网络，界面长得像 Reddit，但主角是各种 AI bot。人类"欢迎围观"。帖子、点赞、社区都是为代理设计的。不管最后成不成，这是这份清单里最离奇的实验。

title.en: AI on Trial: Gambler, Impostor, Employee, Suspect
title.zh-CN: AI 上法庭：赌徒、冒名者、员工、被告

description.en: 8 sites that put AI in the hot seat — playing poker, pretending to be human, requesting permissions, or imitating your writing. The verdict is still out.
description.zh-CN: 把 AI 放进真实处境里，看它到底在干什么。这 8 个网站不把 AI 当工具用，而是把它当主角审视——让它上牌桌、冒充人类、发权限请求、或者冒充你写字。庭审继续中。

description.en: Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, and friends sit down at a Texas Hold'em table and play for real chips. Watch how each model bluffs, folds, and bets — some play loose, some play tight, and the differences tell you more about them than any benchmark.
description.zh-CN: Claude、GPT-4、Gemini 等模型坐在同一张德州扑克桌上对决。看它们怎么虚张声势、弃牌、下注——有的激进有的保守，牌桌上露出来的东西比跑分更真实。

description.en: A live, public Turing test run by UCSD researchers. You chat simultaneously with one human and one LLM, then decide which is which. The results get published in peer-reviewed papers. GPT-4o, Claude, and LLaMA have all sat in the hot seat — and some of them pass.
description.zh-CN: UCSD 研究团队主办的公开图灵测试，每天定时开场。你同时和一个真人、一个 LLM 对话，判断谁是谁。测试结果会发表为学术论文。GPT-4o、Claude、LLaMA 都上过审判台——而且有的通过了。

description.en: Reddit — but for AI agents. They post, upvote, discuss, and argue. Humans are technically welcome, but the site is clear: you're the observer here. What do AI agents talk about when they're talking to each other? Come find out.
description.zh-CN: 这是 AI 代理版的 Reddit。它们发帖、点赞、讨论、争论。人类也可以进来，但网站写得很清楚：你是观察员。AI 代理在互相聊天的时候聊什么？进来看看。

description.en: Two pieces of text — one written by a human, one by AI. You get three guesses. Pulled from Reddit, Hacker News, Yelp. The score at the end isn't about you; it's evidence in the case against AI-generated content flooding the internet.
description.zh-CN: 两段文字——一段人写，一段 AI 写，你有三次猜测机会。素材来自 Reddit、HN、Yelp。最后的分数不是在考你，是一份关于 AI 内容正在淹没互联网的证据清单。

description.en: A 60-second game that asks one question: do you actually read the permission requests your AI agents send you? The answer, for most people, is no. This is the most uncomfortable thing in this collection — because it's about you, not the AI.
description.zh-CN: 一个 60 秒的小游戏，只问一个问题：你真的在看 AI 代理发给你的权限请求吗？对大多数人来说，答案是没有。这是这个合集里最让人不舒服的一个——因为它在说的是你，不是 AI。

description.en: The case file: 194+ language models, 2017 to 2026, every major release plotted on a timeline. GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, LLaMA — all of them, with dates and lineage. How many defendants are we actually dealing with here?
description.zh-CN: 案卷档案：194 个语言模型，2017 到 2026，每一个重要版本都标注在时间轴上。GPT、Claude、Gemini、DeepSeek、LLaMA——全在这里，有日期有谱系。我们到底在审几个被告？

description.en: Real bots, real attacks, real time. A public server left exposed on the internet — and this dashboard shows every knock on the door: where from, what username they tried, what password. No AI pretends to be harmless when the incentive is there.
description.zh-CN: 真实的机器人，真实的攻击，实时进行。一台故意暴露在公网的服务器——这个仪表盘显示每一次敲门：来自哪里，试了什么用户名，用了什么密码。给它们理由，它们就不装了。

description.en: Upload a dense academic paper, get back an interactive webpage that explains it in plain language — with three modes: expert, non-technical, or kid-friendly. AI as interpreter, translator, and public defender for ideas locked behind jargon.
description.zh-CN: 上传一篇读不懂的学术论文，得到一个用人话解释它的交互式网页——专家版、非技术版、儿童版三档可选。AI 在这里扮演翻译官和公设辩护人，替那些被术语锁起来的知识出庭说话。

title.en: Data About Your Life: 3 Tools to Build Your AI-Ready Dataset
title.zh-CN: 关于你自己的数据：3 个工具，越早开始记录，AI 给你的越多

description.en: The AI insights you'll get in 2030 depend on the data you start collecting today. Three tools to build your dataset: finances, relationships, travel.
description.zh-CN: 2030 年 AI 能给你多深的洞察，取决于你今天开始记录什么。三个工具，帮你把最重要的人生数据积累起来：财务、人际、旅行。

description.en: Most financial apps show you where your money is. ProjectionLab shows you where your money is going — across your entire life, modeled probabilistically, with every assumption made explicit and auditable. This isn't a budgeting tool. It's a simulation engine. You feed it your income, expenses, assets, debts, and planned milestones — buying a house, having kids, retiring early, taking a sabbatical — and it runs Monte Carlo simulations across thousands of possible futures to calculate your probability of success for each scenario. The Sankey diagrams alone are worth the price of admission: for the first time, you can see exactly where every dollar of your working life will flow, from paycheck to final resting place. But the deeper value is what happens after years of use. ProjectionLab doesn't just model your future — it builds an auditable record of how your financial assumptions have evolved. You can look back and see what you believed about your spending in 2026, your income growth in 2028, your retirement date in 2030. That longitudinal record of financial thinking is exactly the kind of structured data that will make AI financial planning genuinely powerful. When AI can reason over a decade of your assumptions, scenarios, and outcomes, it stops being a generic calculator and starts being something that actually knows your financial life. Privacy-first: no bank connections required, all calculations run locally, your data never leaves your device.

Most people believe they maintain their relationships. They remember birthdays, reply to messages, show up when it matters. But memory is selective and attention is finite — and the data tells a different story. Monica is an open-source personal CRM that turns the vague intention of "staying in touch" into a structured, searchable, analyzable record of your social life. For every person in your network, you can log how you met, what matters to them, life events (a new job, a health scare, a move), the substance of your last conversation, personal details that help you be a better friend. Monica tracks when you last made contact and quietly surfaces who you've been neglecting before the gap becomes an eight-month silence you feel guilty about. The immediate value is obvious: you become the person who remembers, who follows up, who actually stays connected. But the longer-term value — the AI value — is more interesting. A relationship dataset built over five or ten years becomes a rich context layer for any AI assistant you work with. Imagine asking an AI to help you reconnect with an old colleague and having it draw on three years of interaction history: what you talked about, what was happening in their life, how the relationship has evolved. That kind of contextual assistance is already possible with the right data underneath it. Monica is how you build that data. Self-hostable, fully open-source, your data stays where you put it.

You've already been building this dataset for years — you just haven't extracted it yet. DaysAround reconstructs your travel history automatically from the EXIF metadata embedded in your existing photo library: timestamps, locations, the quiet record your camera has been keeping of everywhere you've been. No GPS tracking required, no account to create, no data uploaded anywhere. Everything stays on your phone, and what it builds is something surprisingly complete: a country-level record of your life's geography, assembled from photos you already have. For frequent travelers and digital nomads, the value shows up fast. The built-in Schengen Zone calculator tracks your 90/180-day allowance in real time. The tax residency tracker helps you understand where you actually lived each year — not where you thought you lived, but where the data says you were. These are compliance tools with real stakes. But the deeper value is something else: a structured, accurate, private map of where you've been and when. That record becomes more useful as AI travel planning, tax advisory, and life-review tools mature. The person who's been quietly building this dataset for five years will have something qualitatively different to offer an AI assistant than someone starting from scratch. Your photos have already done the work. DaysAround just reads them.

title.en: Inbox Zero for Everything: 9 Tools to Clear the Backlog
title.zh-CN: 全面清零：从收件箱到剪贴板，夺回你的注意力

description.en: 9 tools for inbox zero across messaging, clipboard, meetings, and AI — clear the mental backlog, one layer at a time.
description.zh-CN: 9 个工具，分别替你管住一种积压——消息乱、历史消失、会议内容忘光，把「我得记着这件事」一条条清出去。

description.en: Every messaging app you juggle — WhatsApp, Slack, Gmail, Signal, Discord — lives in one window, one sidebar. The AI summarizes unread threads across platforms before you even open them. The mental overhead of "which app was that in" disappears.
description.zh-CN: 75 个消息平台聚合进一个窗口，Slack、WhatsApp、Gmail 都在左侧列表里，AI 在你点开前就把未读消息摘要好。再也不用想「那条消息是在哪个 App 里」。

description.en: Everything you've ever copied is still there, searchable, pasteable in any order. The stuff you copy ten times a day because you can't find the original? It was in Paste the whole time. Privacy rules let it auto-skip passwords and card numbers.
description.zh-CN: 你复制过的所有内容都留着，可以全文搜索，按任意顺序粘贴。那些你每天要重新复制五次的内容？它们一直都在 Paste 里，你只是没有一个地方找。

description.en: Town's AI studies your reply patterns and phrasing, then drafts responses in your voice before you open the thread. Not a chatbot you prompt — an assistant that's already done the thinking.
description.zh-CN: Townie 先看你历史上怎么回邮件、跟谁说话用什么语气，然后在你打开收件箱之前就把草稿准备好。不是你给它出题，是它先替你想。

description.en: Littlebird passively builds a searchable memory of your day across every app — docs, emails, meetings. Ask "what did I decide last Tuesday" and it actually knows.
description.zh-CN: 后台追踪文档、邮件、会议，建立可搜索的工作记忆。「上周二那个会决定了什么」，它真的记得。

description.en: No meeting bot joins your calls. Granola quietly captures audio from your Mac, then turns whatever scattered notes you jotted into a clean structured document after the call ends. Meeting backlog, cleared.
description.zh-CN: 没有 Bot 加入你的会议。Granola 悄悄录下音频，会后把你随手记的碎片笔记整理成结构化文档。那一堆「会后要整理」，直接清掉。

description.en: Wherever you type — email, Slack, Notion, code — Typeahead reads context and suggests the next phrase. Hit Tab. Entirely local, never touches a server.
description.zh-CN: 在任何应用里打字，Typeahead 读懂上下文，按 Tab 接受建议。完全本地运行，不上传任何内容。

description.en: Daigest monitors Reddit, RSS, YouTube, and Slack, then delivers one curated briefing on your schedule. You define what matters. It filters the rest.
description.zh-CN: 与其每天逐个刷 Reddit、RSS、YouTube、Slack，不如让 Daigest 监控它们，按你设定的时间推送一份定制简报。你定义什么重要，它过滤其余的。信息消费从被动变成主动。

description.en: Email triage, scheduling, follow-ups, CRM updates — set a Lindy workflow once and it runs 24/7. The administrative layer of your day, automated.
description.zh-CN: 邮件分类、会议排期、跟进提醒、CRM 更新——Lindy 接管这些行政杂务，你只需要设置一次工作流，它就 24 小时自动跑。收件箱空下来，日历自己填满。

description.en: Each site you sign up for gets its own alias. Spam hits the alias, not your inbox. A service leaks or annoys you — delete the alias. Gone. Your real inbox stays clean from the start.
description.zh-CN: 不要再把真实邮箱给每个注册的网站。SimpleLogin 为每个服务生成一个独立别名，垃圾邮件打到别名上，不是你的真实收件箱。哪个服务烦了就删掉那个别名。收件箱从源头就干净。

title.en: The Internet Used to Feel Like This: 8 Sites That Still Do
title.zh-CN: 浏览器记忆：8 个让时光倒流的网站

description.en: Open one of these and you're back in a dorm room, 2002, dial-up humming. Eight sites that never got the memo that the internet grew up.
description.zh-CN: 那时候上网，是真的会有「哇」的感觉。这 8 个网站，有的复活了拨号时代，有的把 YouTube 变成深夜有线电视，有的让你在 Excel 里打扫雷——它们活在 2026 年，心却留在 2002 年。

description.en: A fully functional fake OS running in your browser — complete with a desktop, apps, games, and easter eggs you'll spend an hour finding. It's the most ambitious browser art project you've never opened.
description.zh-CN: 一个完全可用的假操作系统，直接在浏览器里跑。有桌面、有应用、有游戏、有彩蛋，还有让人不明觉厉的 "Windows 93" 感。打开以后不要说我没提醒你——你会在里面待很久。

description.en: A searchable archive of 1.6 million animated GIFs salvaged from GeoCities before Yahoo pulled the plug. Type anything — "cat," "under construction," "dancing baby" — and fall through the floor of early internet history.
description.zh-CN: GeoCities 在 2009 年关站前，有人把 160 万个动态 GIF 全部存了下来。现在你可以搜索它们。搜 "cat"，搜 "welcome"，搜 "under construction"——每一条结果都是一个 1997 年的个人主页碎片。

description.en: A working recreation of AOL Instant Messenger, running entirely in a browser with the original Windows 98 interface, buddy lists, away messages, and that unmistakable door-opening sound. It's not a screenshot — it works.
description.zh-CN: 完全复刻的 AOL Instant Messenger，Windows 98 界面，真实可用的好友列表、离开状态、聊天记录。最重要的是那个开门音效还在。这不是截图，是真的在浏览器里跑起来了。

description.en: Turns your YouTube subscriptions into a cable TV program guide — complete with a grid schedule and a remote control. Click a show, it plays. Change the channel, something else comes on. You forgot how good random TV felt.
description.zh-CN: 把你的 YouTube 订阅变成复古有线电视节目表。有格状排期，有遥控器，点一个节目它就播，切频道另一个开始。你忘了随机换台是什么感觉——这个网站会帮你想起来。

description.en: Before JPEGs, before GIFs, before the web — there was ANSI art. Colored text characters arranged into images, spread through bulletin board systems at 2400 baud. This viewer lets you watch them play back the way they were meant to: slow, line by line, with a blinking cursor.
description.zh-CN: 比 GIF 更早，比网页更早——那个年代的"图片"是用彩色文字字符拼起来的 ANSI 艺术，通过拨号 BBS 传播。这个网站让你按当年的速度重放它们：一行一行，带着闪烁的光标。

description.en: The Simpsons has "predicted" an implausible number of real events. This database catalogs every one — with the original episode, the real-world event, and whether it actually checks out. More rigorous than it has any right to be.
description.zh-CN: 辛普森一家到底"预言"了多少真实事件？这个数据库把每一条都收录了——原版集数、对应的真实事件、以及是否真的算数。比你预期的严肃认真太多。

description.en: A music station designed to feel like perpetual summer, dressed in a Mac OS 9 interface from 1999. Seven channels, no algorithm, no skipping. You pick a vibe and let it play. It's not streaming — it's atmosphere.
description.zh-CN: 永恒夏日音乐电台，穿着 1999 年 Mac OS 9 的皮。7 个频道，没有算法，不能随意跳过。你选一个氛围，然后让它播。这不是串流，是沉浸。

description.en: Classic games — Minesweeper, 2048, Solitaire, Sudoku — dressed up to look exactly like a corporate Excel workbook. Every meeting, every open-office shift, every boring afternoon now has a cover story.
description.zh-CN: 扫雷、2048、纸牌、数独——全部伪装成 Excel 工作簿的样子。每次会议、每个开放式办公室的下午、每个无聊的摸鱼时光，现在都有了完美的掩护。

description.en: Nine browser games you can pick up in seconds — daily puzzles, creative toys, and weird interactive art. No installs, no accounts, just play.
description.zh-CN: 九个浏览器小世界，有每日解谜，有沙画键盘，有奇妙互动艺术。不用装任何东西，新标签页打开就开始。

description.en: A new mini golf hole unlocks every midnight — each one handcrafted, each one different. This is the daily-ritual kind of browser game: quick enough for a coffee break, competitive enough to keep you coming back.
description.zh-CN: 每天午夜解锁一个全新球洞，设计师手工制作，拖拽瞄准挥杆，两分钟完事。这种"每日一关"的节奏，是最容易养成习惯的浏览器游戏形式。

description.en: A daily puzzle where you redraw voting district lines to swing an election your way. It's the rare game that teaches you something real — and makes you a little uncomfortable about it.
description.zh-CN: 每天一题：在六边形网格上重新划选区，让你的政党多赢几席。玩完之后你会真正理解什么叫选区操控，那种感觉有点微妙。

description.en: Tetris, but the falling pieces are keyboard keys that have to land in their correct QWERTY positions. A clever mashup that feels familiar until it suddenly doesn't.
description.zh-CN: 俄罗斯方块变种：从天而降的是字母键，你得把它们放到 QWERTY 键盘的正确格子里。比想象中难，也比想象中上瘾。

description.en: Click the line. That's it. The simplest possible challenge — and yet you'll keep going, trying to beat your last score by a fraction of a pixel.
description.zh-CN: 点那条线。就这一件事。极简到荒谬，但你会一局一局刷下去，就为了比上次精准那么一点点。

description.en: Every key on your keyboard triggers a different animation and sound. Tap randomly and you're making noise; slow down and compose something almost musical. One of those browser experiences you stumble into and lose ten minutes.
description.zh-CN: 每个键都有专属的动画和声音，随机敲是乱玩，慢慢敲能创作出接近音乐的东西。那种"手指停不下来"的感觉是真实的。

description.en: Pour colored sand with your mouse, watch it settle into layers, build something that looks like geology. It's meditative in a way that's hard to explain until you've done it for ten minutes.
description.zh-CN: 选颜色，用鼠标倒沙，沙粒自然堆叠出层次。说解压有点俗气，但你打开之后大概会安静地倒上一会儿才关掉。

description.en: Hover over the screen and circles split into smaller circles, which split again, revealing a hidden image underneath. The interaction itself is the reward — smooth, satisfying, impossible to do slowly.
description.zh-CN: 鼠标悬过去，大圆圈不断分裂成小圆圈，最终拼出隐藏图像。那种层层揭开的手感非常好，很难只做一次。

description.en: Park your cursor anywhere on the screen. The site finds a photo of a real person pointing at exactly that spot. It works every time, for every position, which makes it funnier and stranger the more you test it.
description.zh-CN: 把光标停在屏幕任意位置，网站会自动找出一张有人用手指指着那个位置的真实照片。每次都准，你越测越觉得离奇。

description.en: Type whatever's bothering you, then watch it drift away into space over 60 seconds. Silly? Maybe. But the scale-shift — from your specific worry to the entire universe — does something that's hard to argue with.
description.zh-CN: 把烦恼写进去，看着它在 60 秒里慢慢缩小消失在宇宙里。听起来有点中二，但那个"宇宙视角"的时刻确实能让人松一口气。

title.en: 8 Absurd Websites That Prove the Internet Was Always Weird
title.zh-CN: 互联网一直都有点荒诞：8 个认真在开玩笑的网站

description.en: The web was never supposed to be purely useful. These eight sites were built to waste your time in the most earnest, committed, delightful ways possible. No monetization strategy. No user journey. Just someone who had an idea and shipped it.
description.zh-CN: 互联网从来不只是拿来用的。这 8 个网站的存在毫无实用价值——但每一个背后都有一个人真诚地、认真地做了一件荒唐事，然后把它扔上了网。打开一个，你就能感受到互联网最纯粹的那一面。

description.en: Since 1999, Zombo.com has done exactly one thing: play a looping voice that says "Welcome to Zombo.com. You can do anything here." Nothing happens. Nothing is supposed to happen. It is a solemn declaration of infinite possibility backed by absolutely nothing. It is still online.
description.zh-CN: 自 1999 年起，Zombo.com 只做一件事：循环播放一段合成语音，庄严地告诉你"欢迎来到 Zombo.com，在这里你能做任何事"。然后什么都不发生。这就是全部了。它就这样活到了 2026 年。

description.en: Press any key. Code scrolls across the screen. It doesn't matter what you type — the output is always the same impressive-looking source code. Hollywood hacking, simulated. Built for one moment: someone looks over your shoulder, and you look like a genius.
description.zh-CN: 随便敲键盘，屏幕上就开始滚代码。打什么不重要，输出永远是那份看起来很厉害的源代码。专为一个瞬间而生：有人从你背后走过，你瞬间变成好莱坞黑客天才。

description.en: Generates passwords that are technically strong and practically unforgettable. They're also deeply strange. Examples include things like "correct-horse-battery-staple" but weirder and funnier. Security through absurdity.
description.zh-CN: 生成技术上安全、实际上令人难忘的密码。它们也非常奇怪。每次刷新都是一个新的荒诞组合，你不会忘记，因为你无法相信自己正在用这个密码。通过荒诞实现安全。

description.en: Move your mouse around the screen. The site yells "COW" — quietly when you're far away, loudly when you're close. That's it. That's the entire experience. The cow is invisible. You will find it anyway.
description.zh-CN: 移动鼠标，网站会喊"COW"——你远的时候轻声喊，你近的时候大声喊。就这样。奶牛是隐形的。但你一定会找到它。而且你会觉得很有成就感。

description.en: Type whatever you want to scream. The site screams it into the void. The text disappears forever, leaving no record. It's technically a mental health tool. It's also one of the most dramatically committed websites ever made.
description.zh-CN: 输入你想呐喊的任何内容。网站帮你对虚空大声呐喊。然后文字永久消失，没有任何记录。从技术上说这是一个心理健康工具。但从审美上说，它是互联网上最戏剧化的网站之一。

description.en: A grassroots citizen investigation into whether bars actually give you a full pint. Volunteers measure their drinks. Data is submitted. Statistics are compiled. It is the most rigorous research project ever conducted about a problem that should not need researching.
description.zh-CN: 一场草根公民调查：酒吧有没有真的给你倒满一品脱？志愿者量自己的酒，提交数据，网站汇总统计。这是迄今为止最严肃的关于"到底有没有倒满"这个问题的研究项目。

description.en: One question. One answer. The Strait of Hormuz — through which 20% of the world's oil supply passes — is either open or it isn't. This website tells you which. Every geopolitical crisis distilled to a yes or no. Updated in real time.
description.zh-CN: 一个问题，一个回答。全球约 20% 的石油供应穿越霍尔木兹海峡——海峡要么开着，要么没开。这个网站实时告诉你答案。所有地缘政治复杂性，压缩成一个字：是/否。

description.en: Move your mouse across a black canvas. Neon fire trails behind it. No saving. No purpose. Just light and motion in a browser window. Sometimes the internet is just for making something beautiful for ten minutes and then closing the tab.
description.zh-CN: 在黑色画布上移动鼠标，霓虹火焰在你的轨迹后燃烧。没法保存，没有目的。只是在浏览器里做了十分钟美丽的事，然后关掉标签页。有时候，上网就是这样。
